plan.action: Read lib/git.sh and tests/git.bats to understand what is already implemented and what is missing relative to the acceptance criteria.
Read clippy.sh and lib/loop.sh to identify where workspace/git checks occur in the startup sequence, ensuring the git repo check is placed before any branch creation or ticket execution.

Implement any missing acceptance criteria in lib/git.sh: (a) git_repo_check — detect if CWD is a git repo; (b) git_init_repo — initialize a new repo with a default main branch on confirmation or auto-init flag; (c) ensure initialization failure exits with a clear error message; (d) wire the check into the startup sequence before branch creation.
Add the missing failure-case test for git_init_repo to tests/git.bats (e.g., git init fails and execution stops with error), and verify all 11 existing tests still pass.
Run shellcheck on lib/git.sh and confirm it exits 0. Fix any warnings or errors reported.
Run the full test suite (bats tests/) and confirm all tests pass with no regressions.

testing_recommendations: Test git_repo_check returns success when CWD contains a .git directory.
Test git_repo_check returns failure and prints a clear warning when CWD has no .git directory.
Test git_init_repo initializes a repo and creates a default main branch when auto-init is enabled.
Test git_init_repo initializes a repo when operator confirmation is provided via stdin.
Test git_init_repo stops execution with a clear error message when git init fails (e.g., stub git to exit non-zero).
Test that the startup sequence invokes git_repo_check before any branch creation function.
Confirm shellcheck on lib/git.sh exits 0 as part of CI or manually in the test run.
risks: The default branch name (main vs master) may conflict with the user's git config — ensure the init step explicitly sets the branch name to 'main'.
Auto-init flag name must be consistent with any existing flag conventions in clippy.sh.
Wiring the check into the startup sequence must not break existing workspace_init ordering assumptions.
